hypothesis: If a 3D electron-density encoder is trained via contrastive alignment to a pose-invariant SMILES encoder without explicit SE(3) constraints, then it will learn rotation-invariant molecular embeddings that outperform unimodal and SE(3)-equivariant baselines on unseen-rotation retrieval and downstream property-based clustering/prediction because the contrastive objective forces different poses of the same molecule to map to a shared representation.
